seoTitle: How to Save Email Attachments to OneDrive With Power Automate
metaDescription: Learn how to automatically save Outlook email attachments to OneDrive using a Power Automate cloud flow.
intro: If you're tired of manually downloading and uploading email attachments to OneDrive, Power Automate can handle it for you automatically. This guide walks through building a flow that saves incoming attachments straight to a OneDrive folder.

steps.heading: Create an automated cloud flow
steps.body: Start a new flow in Power Automate with an automated trigger connected to your Outlook account.

steps.heading: Configure the Outlook email trigger
steps.body: Set the flow to trigger when a new email arrives, adjusting filters for sender, subject, or folder.

steps.heading: Filter emails and extract attachments
steps.body: Add conditions to only process relevant emails, then extract the attachments for saving.

steps.heading: Save files to OneDrive
steps.body: Use the OneDrive create file action to upload each attachment into your chosen folder.

steps.heading: Organize files and avoid duplicates
steps.body: Add logic to sort attachments into folders and check for existing files before saving to prevent duplicate uploads.

faq.question: Can I choose which OneDrive folder attachments go into?
faq.answer: Yes, you specify the destination folder when configuring the OneDrive save action in the flow.

faq.question: Will this work with multiple attachments in one email?
faq.answer: Yes, the flow can loop through each attachment in an email and save them all individually.

faq.question: Can I filter out emails I don't want processed?
faq.answer: Yes, you can filter by sender, subject, or folder so only specific emails trigger the save action.

faq.question: How do I prevent the same attachment from being saved twice?
faq.answer: You can add a check within the flow for existing files with the same name before uploading a new copy.
